mcqs.question: In the minimax algorithm, the MAX player aims to:

options.text: Minimize the utility value at every level
Maximize the utility value assuming the opponent plays optimally
Choose moves randomly to confuse the opponent
Always select the deepest node in the game tree

mcqs.explanation: In the minimax algorithm, the MAX player selects the move that maximizes the utility value, while assuming the opponent (MIN) will also play optimally by choosing moves that minimize the utility value.

mcqs.question: In alpha-beta pruning, a branch is pruned when:

options.text: Alpha is greater than or equal to beta

mcqs.explanation: Alpha-beta pruning prunes a branch when alpha >= beta. Alpha represents the best value the maximizer can guarantee, and beta represents the best value the minimizer can guarantee. When alpha >= beta, the current branch cannot influence the final decision.

mcqs.question: Which of the following games is classified as a two-player, zero-sum, perfect information game?
mcqs.explanation: Chess is a two-player, zero-sum game with perfect information because both players can see the entire board state. Poker and Bridge involve hidden cards (imperfect information), and Backgammon involves dice (stochastic element).

mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of a heuristic evaluation function in game-playing AI?

options.text: To generate all possible legal moves from a position
To estimate the value of non-terminal game positions when the full tree cannot be searched
To determine the exact winner of the game at any state
To prune branches in the game tree automatically

mcqs.explanation: A heuristic evaluation function estimates the desirability of non-terminal game positions. Since it is impractical to search the entire game tree for complex games like chess, the search is cut off at a certain depth and the evaluation function provides an estimate of how good the position is.

mcqs.question: In Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS), the four main steps in order are:
mcqs.explanation: MCTS follows four steps: Selection (traverse tree to a leaf), Expansion (add child nodes), Simulation (random playout to terminal state), and Backpropagation (update statistics along the path). This algorithm was notably used in AlphaGo.
